lede: A look at November 8 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Masashi Kishimoto and Gordon Ramsay, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Louvre Abu Dhabi
events.desc: The Louvre Abu Dhabi was inaugurated by the French president Emmanuel Macron and then-crown prince of Abu Dhabi Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan.

events.title: Typhoon Haiyan
events.desc: Typhoon Haiyan, one of the strongest tropical cyclones ever recorded, strikes the Visayas region of the Philippines; the storm left at least 6,340 people dead with over 1,000 still missing, and caused $2.86 billion (2013 USD; equivalent to $3.95 billion in 2025) in damage.

events.title: Eritrea
events.desc: Eritrea adopts the nakfa as its official currency.

events.title: Massachusetts
events.desc: Former Massachusetts Attorney General Edward Brooke becomes the first African American elected to the United States Senate since Reconstruction.

events.title: Pan Am Flight 7
events.desc: Pan Am Flight 7 disappears between San Francisco and Honolulu. Wreckage and bodies are discovered a week later.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: French Resistance coup in Algiers, in which 400 civilian French patriots neutralize Vichyist XIXth Army Corps after 15 hours of fighting, and arrest several Vichyist generals, allowing the immediate success of Operation Torch in Algiers.

events.title: Greco-Italian War
events.desc: Greco-Italian War: The Italian invasion of Greece fails as outnumbered Greek units repulse the Italians in the Battle of Elaia–Kalamas.

events.title: Shunzhi Emperor
events.desc: The Shunzhi Emperor, the third emperor of the Qing dynasty, is enthroned in Beijing after the collapse of the Ming dynasty as the first Qing emperor to rule over China.

events.title: Monarchy of Sweden
events.desc: After being crowned king of Sweden, Christian II gave the order to execute nearly 100 people, mostly noblemen, despite promises of general amnesty.

events.title: Trần Thánh Tông
events.desc: Trần Thánh Tông, the second emperor of the Trần dynasty, decides to pass the throne to his crown prince Trần Khâm and take up the post of Retired Emperor.

births.name: Masashi Kishimoto
births.line: Japanese author and illustrator, created Naruto

births.name: Gordon Ramsay
births.line: British chef, restaurateur, and television host/personality

births.name: Bram Stoker
births.line: Irish novelist and critic, created Count Dracula

deaths.text: Ivory Joe Hunter, American singer-songwriter and pianist
Ivan Bunin, Russian author and poet, Nobel Prize laureate
Doc Holliday, American dentist and poker player

observances.text: Christian feast day: Saint Elizabeth of the Trinity (Roman Catholic Church)
Christian feast day: Blessed John Duns Scotus
Christian feast day: Saints and Martyrs of England (Church of England)
